title: Iceberg std::out_of_range with insert on nested types
description:
### Describe the bug
Easy to reproduce. I found this while I am still debugging jars with Pyspark 😢
### How to reproduce
Run:
```sql
CREATE TABLE t0 (c0 Map(Tuple(Int),Tuple(Int))) ENGINE = IcebergLocal(local, path = '/var/lib/clickhouse/user_files/lakehouse/t0/');
INSERT INTO TABLE t0 (c0) SELECT c0 FROM generateRandom('c0 Map(Tuple(Int),Tuple(Int))', 903856812385940645, 1, 1) LIMIT 1;
SELECT c0 FROM t0; --std::exception. Code: 1001, type: std::out_of_range, e.what() = unordered_map::at: key not found
```
### Error message and/or stacktrace
Stack trace:
```

```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id INT, data JSON);
INSERT INTO t1 (id, data) VALUES (1, JSON_OBJECT('1_2', JSON_ARRAY('value1', 1.1), '3_4', JSON_ARRAY('value2', 2.2)));
CREATE TABLE t2 (key INT, value VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO t2 (key, value) SELECT id, JSON_UNQUOTE(JSON_EXTRACT(data, '$.1_2[0]')) FROM t1 WHERE JSON_EXTRACT(data, '$.1_2[0]') IS NOT NULL;
SELECT key, value FROM t2 WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Insert into nested Map type in Iceberg table
